description_long: This is a classic hammer featuring a robust wooden handle with a natural grain and a slightly curved ergonomic shape for comfortable grip. The head is metallic and consists of two distinct ends: one flat, cylindrical striking face for driving nails and a rounded, ball-peen end for shaping metal or riveting. The transition between the handle and the head is reinforced, suggesting durability and strength for heavy-duty tasks. The wood appears smooth and polished, while the metal head has a matte, slightly textured finish. No visible branding, markings, or signs of wear are present, indicating the hammer is in pristine condition. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for both construction and metalworking tasks in urban environments.
description: A wooden-handled hammer with a dual-ended metal head, featuring a flat striking face and a rounded ball-peen end. The handle is smooth and slightly curved, and the hammer appears new and unused.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the dual-ended metal head, with both the flat and rounded ends visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the circular cross-section of the handle and the width of the hammer head, with the ball-peen end most prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the alignment of the head, with the flat striking face in the foreground.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the symmetrical design of the head and the handle's attachment point, with both ends of the head clearly visible.
attribute_handle_type: wooden slightly curved handle with a smooth, polished finish and visible natural wood grain
attribute_head_type: ball-peen hammer head with one flat cylindrical striking face and one rounded ball-peen end, both made of steel
